Design system  /  Print  /  Emails  /  Email · order confirmation

Email · order confirmation

Confirmation email after a signed quotation. "What you get" lead-in like email-quotation, but now in full text, three hex-bullet deliverables, kick-off date prominent, contacts at the bottom. Mint-green header because this is "go".

Subject Confirmed: {{ ENGAGEMENT_TITLE }} · kick-off {{ KICKOFF_DATE }} From {{ SENDER_EMAIL }} To {{ CLIENT_EMAIL }}
Confirmed · OC-{{ YEAR }}-{{ NUMBER }}
We're on it, {{ CONTACT_FIRST_NAME }}.

You signed quote {{ QUOTE_NR }}. I'm confirming the agreements below so they sit in one place for both of us.
What we'll deliver
  • {{ DELIVERABLE_1 }}. One line per thing, reader-first.
  • {{ DELIVERABLE_2 }}. Concrete, verb-led.
  • {{ DELIVERABLE_3 }}. Optional; remove if not applicable.
Kick-off
{{ KICKOFF_DATE }}
Final delivery
{{ END_DATE }}
Amount
€ {{ AMOUNT }}
Add kick-off to my calendar →

The full order confirmation is attached to this mail as a PDF. Keep it; we use it as the working document.

Questions, or anything to adjust before we start? Call or mail me directly.

{{ SENDER_NAME }}
{{ SENDER_ROLE }} · Conduction
{{ SENDER_TEL }} · {{ SENDER_EMAIL }}
Conduction B.V. · KvK 76741850 · VAT NL860784241B01 · IBAN NL51 ABNA 0868951550 · Lauriergracht 14h, 1016 RR Amsterdam
Email · order confirmation · mint-green header signals "go"
Why we built it this way

Confirmation sounds different from invoice or reminder

Mint-green header and mint bullet hexes signal "we're here, it's running". Different from email-invoice (cobalt) or email-reminder (orange). The client knows in one glance which phase this mail is.

  • Mint green, not cobalt. Cobalt is brand default; mint is "go / confirmed / stable". Matches the status-badge convention from components.html.
  • Three deliverables, not the full scope list. The PDF has the full set; the email is a summary. Reader scans and knows "yes, that's right".
  • Dates + amount in one strip. Kick-off / end / amount. What the client most wants to know sits on a single line.
  • Kick-off-to-calendar button, not "click here". Concrete action that does something in the world (creates a calendar event).
  • Person at the bottom, human-to-human. No "team@" or noreply. The buyer replies to a human.
600px width · Outlook + Gmail safe · chassis sibling of email-invoice + email-quotation · mint-green follows the status-badge convention · PDF attachment is order-confirmation.html (printed)
Uses
email-invoice chassis · order-confirmation as the attached PDF · quotation as the upstream document · --c-mint-500 for the "go" header · MKB-direct copy from voice
